refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize energy consumption per ton moved to meet or exceed the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the high-level FR into a measurable and actionable sub-FR focused on minimizing energy per ton moved aligns with the acceptance criteria and enables targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure total energy used and tons moved over the evaluation period and verify that energy_per_ton ≤ E_target.
sub_frs: Minimize energy consumption during vehicle operation
Optimize task scheduling to reduce idle and unnecessary movements
Monitor and report energy usage and tonnage moved

candidate_dps: Intelligent task scheduling and routing algorithms
Real-time energy monitoring and reporting system
selected_dp.selected_dp: Combination of energy-efficient vehicle powertrain design, intelligent task scheduling and routing algorithms, and real-time energy monitoring and reporting system

potential_sources: Interaction between vehicle powertrain and task scheduling
Data exchange between monitoring system and vehicle control
coupling_check.notes: The selected DPs are logically coupled but can be modularized with clear interfaces to minimize complexity.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate sensors for energy and tonnage measurement
Risk of suboptimal scheduling algorithms impacting energy efficiency
Risk of integration complexity between vehicle and teleoperation modules
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider iterative refinement of task scheduling algorithms based on operational feedback.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical components responsible for energy-efficient powertrain and sensors for energy and tonnage measurement

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle sends real-time energy usage and tonnage data to Teleoperation and Safety Services

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle receives optimized task schedules from Teleoperation module

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication channel for data exchange between Vehicle and Teleoperation

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of energy efficiency functions; low diffusion risk as powertrain and sensors are physically co-located.
Contains task scheduling algorithms; moderate diffusion risk due to interaction with Vehicle.
Minimal containment; mainly receives telemetry for safety monitoring.
System-level integration; moderate containment and diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy efficiency is best contained within the Vehicle module due to physical integration of powertrain and sensors, minimizing interface complexity and diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential complexity in coordinating task scheduling across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules
Risk of data latency affecting real-time monitoring and control
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Enhance interface definitions and data synchronization protocols between Vehicle and Teleoperation.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well-defined and measurable; selected DPs logically satisfy the FRs with manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Moderate diffusion risk between Vehicle and Teleoperation due to task scheduling and telemetry exchange
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment within Vehicle for powertrain and sensors is strong; task scheduling logically assigned to Teleoperation.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring energy and tonnage data from Vehicle telemetry and comparing against targets.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of energy efficiency functions to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Refine interface protocols to reduce latency and improve data integrity
Iterate on task scheduling algorithms based on operational data

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, interface and integration risks warrant refinement before proceeding to next level.
next_level_sub_frs: Minimize energy consumption during vehicle operation
Optimize task scheduling to reduce idle and unnecessary movements
Monitor and report energy usage and tonnage moved

revise.why: To improve interface definitions and data synchronization between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to mitigate diffusion risks.

assumptions: Accurate sensors are available and reliable
Network link supports required data rates with low latency
open_risks: Integration complexity between Vehicle and Teleoperation
Potential data latency impacting real-time control
evidence_needed: Sensor accuracy and reliability test results
Network performance metrics under operational conditions
